summary: Parallel read-only extraction of vanilla 26.1 texture-UV layouts for 17 colored-only bbb entity models

cubes.note: Vanilla: texOffs(0,0).addBox(0,-2.5,-2.5, 0,5,5) on the 'back' part with PartPose.offsetAndRotation(-11,0,0, PI/4,0,0).withScale(0.8). The 0.8 part-scale is BAKED into the bbb cube: 5x5 plane -> 4x4 and centre -2.5 -> -2.0. UV-unwrap dims passed to addBox are the un-scaled [0,5,5] (UV is computed from addBox dims, not the post-scale geometry). Zero-thickness YZ plane (dx=0) -> both faces are unwrapped. The whole mesh is additionally scaled 0.9 via mesh.transformed (lives in arrow_model_root_transform), which does not affect UVs.

Vanilla: shared 'cross' builder texOffs(0,0).addBox(-12,-2,0, 16,4,0, CubeDeformation.NONE, 1.0F, 0.8F). The trailing 1.0F,0.8F are the texture-scale-u / texture-scale-v args (CubeListBuilder.addBox overload), NOT geometry inflation (deformation is NONE). So bbb_size == uv_size == [16,4,0]; the v texture-scale of 0.8 is a UV-coordinate scale on the unwrap that the deferred texture path must honour. Zero-thickness XY plane (dz=0) -> both faces unwrapped. Part pose rotation PI/4.

Reuses the same vanilla 'cross' CubeListBuilder as cross_1 (texOffs(0,0).addBox(-12,-2,0,16,4,0, NONE, 1.0F, 0.8F)); only the part pose rotation differs (3*PI/4 vs PI/4). Identical tex_offs/uv_size to cross_1. bbb reuses ARROW_CROSS_CUBES for both crossed planes.

specs.issues: Single bbb variant `Arrow` (no baby/variant fields in catalog.rs or selection.rs key "arrow"), so no baby_atlas_size. Atlas 32x32 from LayerDefinition.create(mesh.transformed(...), 32, 32). All 3 bbb cubes match vanilla addBox 1:1 by offset+dims (after un-baking the 0.8 scale on 'back'); part cube counts match (1+1+1). TEXTURE-SCALE SUBTLETY: the cross planes use the addBox overload addBox(ox,oy,oz,dx,dy,dz,CubeDeformation.NONE,1.0F,0.8F) where 1.0F/0.8F are per-box texture-coordinate u/v scale factors (the unwrapped UV rect is scaled by 0.8 in v) — the application's UV unwrap must apply this 0.8 v-scale or the fletching UVs will be wrong; uv_size reports the un-scaled addBox dims [16,4,0]. SCALE-BAKING SUBTLETY: 'back' uses PartPose.withScale(0.8) which bbb baked into the cube geometry (0/5/5 -> 0/4/4, -2.5 -> -2.0); the UV must still be computed from the original addBox dims [0,5,5], hence uv_size=[0,5,5] though bbb_size=[0,4,4]. Whole-mesh 0.9 scale (mesh.transformed) is geometry-only, no UV effect, and is handled by arrow_model_root_transform in bbb. TEXTURE VARIANT SELECTION is runtime, not geometric: TippableArrowRenderer picks arrow.png (normal) vs arrow_tipped.png (entity.getColor()>0), SpectralArrowRenderer always uses arrow_spectral.png. bbb collapses these to one model variant; I listed all three texture paths with variant_key default/tipped/spectral so the textured path can wire whichever the entity selects. Two non-default colour tints exist on the colored debug path (ARROW_HEAD for 'back', ARROW_SHAFT for the crosses) — irrelevant to UV but noted because the same parts carry both tint and texture. All boxes are zero-thickness planes (dx=0 for back, dz=0 for crosses) so both faces share the same unwrap region.

specs.issues: Every bbb cube maps 1:1 to a vanilla addBox by exact offset+dims; no mirror() is active anywhere in either model. ATLAS: adult 64x64 (LayerDefinition.create(mesh,64,64)), baby 32x32 (LayerDefinition.create(mesh,32,32)) — separate texture sets/sizes. TEXTURE/VARIANTS: AxolotlRenderer maps each of 5 Axolotl.Variant (lucy/wild/gold/cyan/blue) to an adult path textures/entity/axolotl/axolotl_<name>.png and a baby path textures/entity/axolotl/axolotl_<name>_baby.png; getTextureLocation picks baby vs adult by isBaby. DEFAULT/colored-debug renders lucy (Variant.DEFAULT=LUCY). bbb's EntityModelKind::Axolotl{baby} only distinguishes adult/baby, NOT color — variant_key 'default' is the lucy adult; I also listed all 5 colors x (adult/baby) so the textured path can wire variant->path. DEFORMATION: adult head + all gill planes + all leg planes carry CubeDeformation(0.001) ('fudge'), which bbb baked into bbb_min/bbb_size (the -0.001 origins, +0.002 sizes); uv_size reported is the UN-inflated dx,dy,dz (8x5x5 head, 8x3x0 / 3x7x0 gills, 3x5x0 legs). Adult body cube1/cube2 and adult tail have NO deformation. All baby boxes use CubeDeformation(0.0) (no inflate). SHARED BUILDERS (adult): vanilla builds one 'rightLeg' CubeListBuilder (origin -2, texOffs(2,13)) reused for BOTH right_front_leg and right_hind_leg, and one 'leftLeg' (origin -1, texOffs(2,13)) reused for BOTH left_front_leg and left_hind_leg — so all four adult legs share texOffs(2,13); bbb mirrors this via two shared cube consts (ADULT_AXOLOTL_RIGHT_LEG_CUBES / LEFT_LEG_CUBES). BABY right_hind_leg: the cube is NOT on the right_hind_leg part itself (a bare doubly-rotated pivot) but on its nested child 'right_leg_r1'; I report it under part_name 'right_hind_leg' to match bbb's part enumeration — its cube const is BABY_AXOLOTL_RIGHT_HIND_LEG_CUBES at texOffs(20,14), same offset as left_hind_leg. BABY leg texOffs differ per leg: right_front=(20,16), right_hind/left_hind=(20,14), left_front=(20,13). PART ORDER: adult emitted in bbb file/hierarchy order (body, head, top/left/right gills, right_front/left_front/right_hind/left_hind legs, tail); baby in bbb order (body, right_front/right_hind/left_front/left_hind legs, tail, head, left/right/top gills). No discrepancies — part cube counts equal the bbb file counts (adult body=2, all others=1; baby body=2, all others=1).

specs.issues: All 16 bbb cubes match the vanilla CreakingModel.createMesh() addBox calls exactly by offset==[min] AND dims==[size], in file order, part-by-part. No discrepancies. NO mirror() is ever called in CreakingModel, so every cube has mirror=false. NO CubeDeformation/inflate is applied to any box, so uv_size == bbb_size for all cubes. Single texture variant only (the bbb variant Self::Creaking carries no fields; selection.rs maps it to the bare key "creaking"); variant_key reported as "default" per instructions. There is NO separate baby layer (no createBabyLayer/createBabyBodyLayer exists), so baby_atlas_size is left empty. COMPOSITE/EMISSIVE OVERLAY: the renderer (CreakingRenderer) adds a second emissive layer via LivingEntityEmissiveLayer using a SEPARATE texture textures/entity/creaking/creaking_eyes.png, built from createEyesLayer() which retains ONLY the 'head' part (mesh.getRoot().retainExactParts(Set.of(\"head\"))) at the SAME 64x64 atlas with the SAME head texOffs. It renders only when state.eyesGlowing (entity active / glowing eyes), with RenderTypes::eyes (additive glow). This eyes overlay is deferred in the bbb colored-only port and is NOT included in the textures list above; if/when added it would be a head-only emissive pass reusing the identical head UVs. Flat planes (head antlers dz=0, both feet dy=0) are zero-thickness quads — their UV unwrap uses the given dx/dy/dz with one dimension 0.

specs.issues: Every bbb cube matched a vanilla addBox exactly by offset AND dims; no mismatches, no extra/missing cubes. Single texture, no baby layer, no variants: variant_key "default" -> textures/entity/enderdragon/dragon.png; atlas 256x256 from LayerDefinition.create(mesh, 256, 256). MIRROR DETAIL (verify in application): vanilla uses CubeListBuilder.mirror() which TOGGLES a sticky flag, applied to all subsequently-added boxes. (a) head: upperlip & upperhead mirror=false, then .mirror() -> scale(-5) & nostril(-5) mirror=true, then a 2nd .mirror() -> scale(3) & nostril(3) back to mirror=false. (b) left_wing & left_wing_tip CubeListBuilders both call .mirror() first, so BOTH boxes (bone + skin membrane) are mirror=true. (c) right_wing & right_wing_tip call NO .mirror(), so both boxes mirror=false. The left/right wings are genuine mirrored geometry (left extends +X from min 0, right extends -X from min -56), so left mirror=true / right mirror=false is correct, NOT a redundant flag. MEMBRANE PLANES: the four "skin" boxes are 56x0x56 zero-thickness planes with NEGATIVE u texOffs (left/right wing skin = [-56,88], wing-tip skin = [-56,144]). The negative u is intentional vanilla UV (the flat plane wraps off the left atlas edge); the application must preserve the negative value, not clamp to 0. In bbb these membrane cubes use DRAGON_MEMBRANE color (lighter tint) vs DRAGON_BODY (dark) for the rest. SHARED LEG GEOMETRY: bbb defines each leg/tip/foot cube ONCE (DRAGON_FRONT_LEG_CUBES, etc.) and reuses it for both the left (+X body pivot) and right (-X body pivot) instances via the body children list; vanilla emits 6 boxes each (3 front-pair + 3 hind-pair x2) but all left/right pairs share identical texOffs and geometry (centered boxes), so a single tex_offs per bbb cube is exact. The bbb cube count per part therefore equals the bbb file (1 cube each for the 6 leg parts). SHARED SPINE: all 5 neck + 12 tail segments reuse DRAGON_SPINE_CUBES (box texOffs [192,104], scale texOffs [48,0]) - identical for every segment in vanilla too. DEFERRED (not texture-relevant): the fully procedural setupAnim (flight-history neck/tail placement, wing flap, jaw open, root bounce/z=-48/xRot), the dying dissolve overlay (dragon_exploding.png), the emissive eyes overlay layer (dragon_eyes.png, RenderTypes.eyes), and the crystal-healing beam (end_crystal_beam.png) are separate render passes/textures not part of the base body UV mapping - only dragon.png maps to the geometry above.

SHARED cube reused for all four baby-donkey legs but vanilla gives each a DISTINCT texOffs: left_hind=(12,44), right_hind=(0,44), left_front=(12,33), right_front=(0,33). bbb has ONE BABY_DONKEY_LEG const referenced 4× in BABY_DONKEY_BODY_CHILDREN order [left_hind, right_hind, left_front, right_front]. tex_offs recorded here is (12,44) for the first (left_hind) instance — see issues for the per-instance UV list the application must apply by part-instance, not by cube const

SHARED cube reused for both baby-donkey ears with DIFFERENT vanilla UV/mirror: left_ear texOffs(0,0) NO mirror; right_ear texOffs(22,0) WITH mirror() (then mirror(false)). bbb has ONE BABY_DONKEY_EAR const referenced 2× in BABY_DONKEY_HEAD_CHILDREN order [left_ear, right_ear]. tex_offs here is left_ear (0,0); the right_ear instance needs tex_offs(22,0) + mirror=true — see issues

specs.issues: See above — comprehensive. Confidence high: all 30 bbb cubes matched a unique vanilla addBox by exact offset+dims with verified texOffs from AbstractEquineModel/BabyHorseModel/DonkeyModel/BabyDonkeyModel; atlas 64x64 confirmed in every LayerDefinition.create. Only nuance is shared-cube per-instance UV for the 4 baby-donkey legs and 2 baby-donkey ears (detailed in the per-cube notes and issues).

cubes.note: Vanilla base.texOffs(0,0).addBox(0,0,0, 10,12,10). Part pivot offset(-5,24,-5). No CubeDeformation.
Shared jaw CubeListBuilder texOffs(40,0).addBox(0,0,0, 4,14,8). upper_jaw child pivot offsetAndRotation(6.5,0,1, 0,0,2.042035). No mirror, no deformation.

Same shared jaw box texOffs(40,0).addBox(0,0,0, 4,14,8). lower_jaw child pivot offsetAndRotation(3.5,0,9, 0,PI,4.2411504). Same UV as upper_jaw (vanilla reuses one CubeListBuilder for both jaws); no mirror, no deformation.

specs.issues: Single texture, no baby layer (no createBabyLayer; baby_atlas_size omitted). The bbb EntityModelKind::EvokerFangs is a unit variant with no parameters, so variant_key is "default". Both jaws share ONE vanilla CubeListBuilder (texOffs(40,0), box 0,0,0,4,14,8) — they are the same UV-unwrapped geometry, distinguished only by part pivot/rotation, so upper_jaw and lower_jaw cubes map to identical tex_offs [40,0]. The bbb file structures this as a single top-level `base` part with two rotated children; I emitted 3 parts (base, upper_jaw, lower_jaw) to keep one cube per part in file order with distinct pivots. No mirror() is active in createBodyLayer for any box. No CubeDeformation/inflate. EvokerFangsRenderer applies a distinct yaw Ry(90 - yRot), standard scale(-1,-1,1) flip, and translate(0,-1.501,0); setupAnim's bite/scale/drop are deferred and irrelevant to UV. Texture path uses default namespace 'minecraft'."

specs.issues: Every bbb cube matched a vanilla addBox 1:1 by offset AND box dims; no unmatched cubes. Cube counts match the bbb file exactly: adult head=4, body=1, tail1=1, tail2=1, each of 4 legs=1 (total 11); baby head=4, body=1, tail1=1, four legs=1 each, tail2=0 cubeless (total 10). NO box anywhere in the feline meshes calls .mirror(), so every mirror flag is false; bbb's left/right legs literally re-use the same shared CubeListBuilder (adult) or use distinct per-leg texOffs (baby), never a mirror. DEFORMATION: adult tail2 uses CubeDeformation(-0.02) (tail_g) -> bbb pre-baked it into bbb_min/bbb_size (min +0.02, size -0.04); uv_size MUST stay the un-inflated [1,8,1] (the integer dx,dy,dz passed to addBox), as reported. The adult collar layer (felineCollarLayer = same mesh with COLLAR_DEFORMATION 0.01, atlas 64x32) and BabyCatModel COLLAR_TRANSFORMER scaling(1.01) are a SEPARATE overlay layer (CatCollarLayer), not part of the base body texture -- ignore for base UV but note it exists. SCALE: the adult cat applies AdultCatModel.CAT_TRANSFORMER = scaling(0.8) in the root transform; ocelot and both babies are unscaled. This scale does NOT change UV. TEXTURE multiplicity: the ocelot has fixed textures (adult textures/entity/cat/ocelot.png, baby ocelot_baby.png from OcelotRenderer). The CAT is a multi-breed entity: CatRenderState.texture comes from entity.getVariant().value().assetInfo(isBaby).texturePath(), resolving to textures/entity/cat/cat_<breed>.png (11 breeds: tabby, black, red, siamese, british_shorthair, calico, persian, ragdoll, white, jellie, all_black) and cat_<breed>_baby.png for babies. I listed cat_tabby/cat_tabby_baby as the representative for variant_keys feline_cat/feline_cat_baby since bbb's variant_key only encodes cat-vs-ocelot and adult-vs-baby (not the breed); the application must pick the actual breed texture at runtime from the cat variant. The adult head 'main'/skull box and the body box are the only adult boxes whose texOffs comes from the running builder state (skull=(0,0) default, body=(20,0) via explicit texOffs() call); all others pass explicit u,v in addBox.

specs.issues: Every bbb cube has a clean 1:1 vanilla match by offset==bbb_min and dims==bbb_size; no extra/missing cubes. Cube counts match the bbb file exactly: adult head 1, right_ear 1, left_ear 1, nose 1, body 1, tail 1, four legs 1 each (= 10 cubes across the adult hierarchy as the file's doc comment states); baby head 4, four legs 1 each, body 1, tail 1 (= 10 cubes). KEY SUBTLETIES the application needs: (1) LEG TEXTURES ARE NOT MIRRORED. bbb stores a SINGLE shared leg cube const (adult FOX_LEG_CUBES, baby BABY_FOX_LEG_CUBES) referenced by all four leg parts, but vanilla builds left vs right legs from TWO DIFFERENT CubeListBuilders with DIFFERENT texOffs and mirror=false: adult right legs (right_hind/right_front) = texOffs(13,24), left legs (left_hind/left_front) = texOffs(4,24); baby right legs = texOffs(22,4), left legs = texOffs(22,0). So when migrating you must give the right-side and left-side leg parts different tex_offs even though they share the same geometry const. (2) ADULT LEG DEFORMATION: vanilla adult legs apply CubeDeformation(0.001) (the 'fudge'); uv_size is the un-inflated [2,6,2] while bbb_min/bbb_size already bake the inflation (min -=0.001 -> 1.999/0.499/-1.001, size +=0.002 -> 2.002/6.002/2.002). Baby legs and all other boxes use CubeDeformation(0.0) (no inflation) — uv_size == bbb_size there. (3) TEXTURE SELECTION is multi-axis and NOT encoded in the bbb EntityModelKind::Fox{baby}. The renderer (FoxRenderer.getTextureLocation) picks the png from variant (Fox.Variant RED/SNOW) x age (adult/baby, =the bbb baby flag) x state (idle vs isSleeping). 8 textures total, listed in `textures`. bbb only encodes adult vs baby (variant_key 'fox'/'fox_baby'); red/snow and idle/sleep variant textures are deferred per the catalog comment, so a textured migration must add variant+sleep plumbing or default to red-idle (textures/entity/fox/fox.png for adult, fox_baby.png for baby). (4) ADULT uses atlas 48x32, BABY uses atlas 32x32 — different UV atlas dimensions per layer; the baby head/legs share the 32x32 atlas. (5) The held-item layer (FoxHeldItemLayer) and the renderer setupRotations pounce/faceplant body-PITCH flip are separate render concerns, not part of this UV mapping.

cubes.note: Exact match: vanilla createBodyLayer() -> root child "knot" CubeListBuilder.create().texOffs(0,0).addBox(-3.0,-8.0,-3.0, 6.0,8.0,6.0) at PartPose.ZERO. No CubeDeformation/inflate, mirror not set.

specs.issues: Single static part with one cube; clean 1:1 match between bbb and vanilla (offset [-3,-8,-3] and dims [6,8,6] identical). No baby layer exists (only createBodyLayer); baby_atlas_size left empty. Only one texture variant -> variant_key "default" -> textures/entity/lead_knot/lead_knot.png (note the vanilla path uses folder/name "lead_knot", not "leash_knot"). LeashKnotRenderer applies poseStack.scale(-1,-1,1) before submitting the model (the model flip already captured by bbb's leash_knot_model_root_transform per the file header); this is a root transform, not a per-cube concern. The bbb catalog encodes this as EntityModelKind::LeashKnot => "leash_knot" in selection.rs (no separate catalog.rs file; no per-variant texture map). No CubeDeformation, no overlay/composite texture, no mirror.

specs.issues:
Every bbb cube matched a unique vanilla addBox cleanly (offset + un-inflated dims), in file order; cube counts match per part (shell 3, body 2, each mouth 1, for both layers).

CubeDeformation handling: the four mouth boxes that bbb stores with fractional min/size (adult upper [-4.999,-1.999,0.001]/[9.998,3.998,3.998], adult lower [-4.999,-1.979,0.001]/[9.998,...], baby upper/lower [-2.499,-0.999,0.001]/[4.998,1.998,1.998]) are vanilla CubeDeformation(-0.001) boxes. bbb pre-bakes the inflate into min(+0.001 per axis)/size(-0.002 per axis). The UV-unwrap dimensions stay the UN-inflated addBox dims, so uv_size is 10x4x4 (adult mouths) / 5x2x2 (baby upper/lower mouths). inner_mouth (both layers) is undeformed (CubeDeformation(0.0)), uv_size == size.

Adult vs baby lower_mouth y-offset asymmetry: adult lower_mouth uses vanilla addBox oy=-1.98 (bbb min y -1.979 = -1.98+0.001), while adult upper_mouth uses oy=-2.0 (bbb min y -1.999). Baby upper AND lower both use oy=-1.0 (bbb min y -0.999). This is faithfully reflected in bbb_min and is just a vanilla geometry quirk, not a UV concern.

Texture selection is by isBaby only (NautilusRenderer.getTextureLocation): adult -> textures/entity/nautilus/nautilus.png, baby -> textures/entity/nautilus/nautilus_baby.png. The bbb EntityModelKind::Nautilus { baby } encodes these as variant keys "nautilus" (baby:false) and "nautilus_baby" (baby:true) per selection.rs:282-283. No per-variant texture map (no ZombieNautilus / coral variants involved here — those are a separate model/renderer).

Overlay/equipment layers NOT covered by the base nautilus.png atlas (and not part of this model's mesh): the saddle (NautilusSaddleModel, textures/entity/equipment/nautilus_saddle) and body armor (NautilusArmorModel, textures/entity/equipment/nautilus_body) are rendered as SimpleEquipmentLayer overlays with their own models/atlases. Not needed for the base textured migration.

No mirror() calls appear anywhere in createBodyMesh or createBabyBodyLayer — all cubes mirror=false.

Zero-thickness rear-fin plane cubes (shell box 3 and body box 2, both layers, dz=0) are valid single-quad planes in vanilla; tex_offs recorded as-is.

specs.vanilla_model_class: PandaModel (adult) / BabyPandaModel (baby), net.minecraft.client.model.animal.panda; both extend QuadrupedModel<PandaRenderState>

specs.issues:
Every bbb cube matched a vanilla addBox exactly by offset AND dims; no offset/size mismatches; no CubeDeformation/inflate anywhere (all uv_size == bbb_size). No mirror() is active on any box in either layer.

CRITICAL leg sharing discrepancy (affects how the application must wire texOffs): bbb stores legs as a SINGLE shared cube const reused by 4 separate ModelPart leg parts (panda_legs builds right_hind/left_hind/right_front/left_front from one `cubes` slice). Vanilla differs by layer:
- ADULT: all 4 legs share ONE CubeListBuilder with texOffs(40,0) -> correct to give every adult leg part the same tex_offs (40,0). The single bbb PANDA_LEG_CUBES const is faithful.
- BABY: vanilla gives each leg its OWN texOffs: right_hind(0,34), left_hind(12,34), right_front(0,29), left_front(12,29). bbb shares ONE BABY_PANDA_LEG_CUBES const, so the per-leg tex_offs CANNOT come from a single shared cube descriptor. The application MUST override tex_offs per baby-leg part (values listed above) rather than reuse one cube's tex_offs for all four. I emitted each baby leg part with its distinct vanilla tex_offs.

Ear UV note: ADULT ears share one UV region texOffs(52,25) for BOTH ears with NO mirror (both sample identical pixels). BABY ears use two distinct regions (24,0) and (33,0), also no mirror.

Variant/texture mapping: bbb EntityModelKind::Panda only carries `baby: bool` (selection.rs keys "panda"/"panda_baby") and does NOT encode the color Gene. Vanilla PandaRenderer keys textures by Panda.Gene (NORMAL/LAZY/WORRIED/PLAYFUL/BROWN/WEAK/AGGRESSIVE) x baby, 14 textures total. Since bbb has no gene field, only the NORMAL gene textures apply: adult -> textures/entity/panda/panda.png, baby -> textures/entity/panda/panda_baby.png. The other 12 gene textures (panda_lazy.png, panda_worried.png, panda_playful.png, panda_brown.png, panda_weak.png, panda_aggressive.png and their lazy_panda_baby.png/worried_panda_baby.png/playful_panda_baby.png/brown_panda_baby.png/weak_panda_baby.png/aggressive_panda_baby.png baby counterparts) are unreachable until bbb adds a gene variant field.

Declaration-order note (already honored by bbb): adult lists head FIRST then body; baby lists body FIRST then head. Cube counts match the bbb file exactly per part (head=4, body=1, each leg=1).

specs.issues: Every bbb cube maps 1:1 to a vanilla addBox by exact offset+dims match; no CubeDeformation/inflate, no mirror() anywhere, atlas 32x32 from LayerDefinition.create(mesh, 32, 32). No baby layer exists (only createBodyLayer; baby_atlas_size left at [0,0]). COMPOSITE/VARIANT NOTE: parrot has 5 color variants (Parrot.Variant RED_BLUE/BLUE/GREEN/YELLOW_BLUE/GRAY) — each is a SEPARATE full texture (not an overlay), selected in ParrotRenderer.getVariantTexture by state.variant; same UV layout for all five. NOTE the GRAY variant maps to file parrot_grey.png (British spelling), and the bbb enum elsewhere spells it 'gray' — variant_key 'gray' points to parrot_grey.png. SHARED-BUILDER NOTE: both wings share texOffs(19,8) and both legs share texOffs(14,18) (vanilla reuses one CubeListBuilder per pair without mirror()), so left/right of each pair sample identical UVs — the visual left/right difference comes only from the part pivot X sign (and the wings' yRot=-PI flip), not from UV mirroring. BBB ENCODING NOTE: bbb's EntityModelKind::Parrot is a single unit variant with no baby/color field, so there is no per-variant key in bbb's selection — strictly the colored debug path renders one body tint + beak tint. The variant_key strings above ('red_blue'/'blue'/'green'/'yellow_blue'/'gray') describe the vanilla texture set the textured path must wire up; if bbb cannot yet distinguish variants, use variant_key 'default' -> the conventional default texture parrot_red_blue.png (vanilla Parrot.Variant default is RED_BLUE).

Vanilla tail texOffs(20,16).
Vanilla head texOffs(0,16).

bbb's RIGHT_EAR child uses RABBIT_RIGHT_EAR_POSE pivot offset [1.5,...], which equals vanilla's LEFT_EAR pivot offset(1.5,...) -> texOffs(32,0). Shared RABBIT_EAR_CUBES box; aligned by pivot. Naming differs from vanilla (bbb 'right' = vanilla 'left').

bbb's LEFT_EAR child uses RABBIT_LEFT_EAR_POSE pivot offset [-1.5,...], which equals vanilla's RIGHT_EAR pivot offset(-1.5,...) -> texOffs(26,0). Shared RABBIT_EAR_CUBES box; aligned by pivot. Naming differs from vanilla.

bbb LEFT_HAUNCH child (RABBIT_LEFT_HAUNCH_POSE yRot -0.3927) matches vanilla left_haunch texOffs(36,24) (yRot -0.3927). Shared RABBIT_HAUNCH_CUBES box.

bbb baby left_ear (BABY_RABBIT_LEFT_EAR_POSE offset [1.5,...]) matches vanilla left_ear texOffs(24,0) offset(1.5,...). Shared BABY_RABBIT_EAR_CUBES box.

bbb baby left_front_leg (built first; left_front_leg_r1) matches vanilla left_front_leg_r1 texOffs(18,8).addBox(-0.5,-1.5,-0.5, 1,3,1). Shared BABY_RABBIT_FRONT_LEG_CUBES box; aligned by build order/parent pivot [1,1,-0.5].
bbb baby right_front_leg (built second; right_front_leg_r1) matches vanilla right_front_leg_r1 texOffs(14,8). Shared BABY_RABBIT_FRONT_LEG_CUBES box; parent pivot [-1,1,-0.5].
bbb baby left_hind_leg's haunch (BABY_RABBIT_LEFT_HAUNCH_POSE offset [1,0,0.5] yRot -0.7854) matches vanilla left_haunch texOffs(10,17). Shared BABY_RABBIT_HAUNCH_CUBES box; aligned by pivot/yaw.
bbb baby right_hind_leg's haunch (BABY_RABBIT_RIGHT_HAUNCH_POSE offset [0.5,0,-0.9] yRot +0.7854) matches vanilla right_haunch texOffs(0,17). Shared BABY_RABBIT_HAUNCH_CUBES box.

specs.issues:
Every bbb cube maps 1:1 to a vanilla addBox by offset+dims; no CubeDeformation/inflate anywhere, so uv_size == bbb_size for all cubes. No mirror() is active on any box (adult or baby).

SHARED-CUBE / NAMING CAVEAT (load-bearing for the app): bbb stores ONE ModelCubeDesc const for the ear, and (in baby) one for the front-leg and one for the haunch, reused by both sides — but vanilla gives left vs right DIFFERENT texOffs. The two sides therefore need DIFFERENT tex_offs even though their bbb_min/bbb_size are identical. I disambiguated each side by its pivot pose (PartPose offset / yaw sign), since the bbb cube geometry is symmetric. Also note bbb's adult ear naming is INVERTED vs vanilla: bbb's 'right_ear' child carries pivot offset +1.5 (vanilla's 'left_ear', texOffs 32,0) and bbb's 'left_ear' child carries pivot -1.5 (vanilla's 'right_ear', texOffs 26,0). Adult front legs are distinct boxes (right addBox -0.9,-1,-0.9 texOffs 36,18; left addBox -1,-1,-1 texOffs 44,18) so they self-disambiguate. Adult haunches: bbb right (yRot +0.3927)->texOffs(20,24), bbb left (yRot -0.3927)->texOffs(36,24). Baby legs: bbb builds left_front_leg before right_front_leg; vanilla left_front_leg_r1=texOffs(18,8), right=texOffs(14,8). Baby haunches: bbb left (offset [1,0,0.5])->texOffs(10,17), bbb right (offset [0.5,0,-0.9])->texOffs(0,17).

TEXTURE SELECTION: bbb's EntityModelKind::Rabbit only encodes { baby: bool } (variant_key 'rabbit' / 'rabbit_baby' in catalog) — it does NOT carry the Rabbit.Variant color or the 'Toast' easter-egg flag. Vanilla RabbitRenderer.getTextureLocation picks from a 7-entry Rabbit.Variant enum map (BROWN/WHITE_SPLOTCHED/EVIL(rabbit_caerbannog)/WHITE/GOLD/BLACK/SALT), plus a Toast override (rabbit_toast / rabbit_toast_baby) when the entity's custom name is 'Toast'. All 7 color variants + toast are listed in `textures` for both adult and baby. The bbb colored debug path renders one brown tint (RABBIT_BROWN); when wiring the textured path, the default/representative texture is the BROWN variant (textures/entity/rabbit/rabbit_brown.png adult, rabbit_brown_baby.png baby) unless bbb is extended to carry the variant. The original entity Rabbit.getVariant() determines which at runtime; bbb currently has no field for it.

Adult atlas 64x64; baby atlas 32x32 (separate baby layer with its own deeper _r1-nested hierarchy). Part/cube counts match the bbb file exactly: 9 adult cubes across 9 leaf parts, 9 baby cubes across 9 leaf parts.

cubes.note: ShulkerModel.createShellMesh: texOffs(0,0).addBox(-8,-16,-8, 16,12,16). No CubeDeformation, no mirror. Exact match to bbb SHULKER_LID_CUBES.
ShulkerModel.createShellMesh: texOffs(0,28).addBox(-8,-8,-8, 16,8,16). No CubeDeformation, no mirror. Exact match to bbb SHULKER_BASE_CUBES.
ShulkerModel.createBodyLayer: texOffs(0,52).addBox(-3,0,-3, 6,6,6). No CubeDeformation, no mirror. Exact match to bbb SHULKER_HEAD_CUBES.

specs.issues: All 3 bbb cubes match vanilla addBox 1:1 by offset AND dims; tex_offs read directly from ShulkerModel (lid 0,0; base 0,28; head 0,52). Atlas is 64x64 (LayerDefinition.create(mesh, 64, 64)). NO BABY LAYER: ShulkerModel exposes only createBodyLayer() (entity: lid+base+head) and createBoxLayer() (block-entity shell-only, lid+base, no head) — both 64x64. There is no createBabyLayer/createBabyBodyLayer and the shulker is not age-scalable, so baby_atlas_size is reported as [64,64] purely as a duplicate of adult (no separate baby geometry/atlas exists); migration should treat shulker as adult-only. TEXTURE VARIANT MODEL: bbb's EntityModelKind::Shulker is a UNIT variant (selection.rs line 92: `Self::Shulker => \"shulker\"`) — it does NOT encode color in the model kind. Color is a per-entity render-state field (ShulkerRenderState.color, a nullable DyeColor) resolved at draw time via ShulkerRenderer.getTextureLocation(color): null -> DEFAULT (textures/entity/shulker/shulker.png), else TEXTURE_LOCATION[color.getId()] -> textures/entity/shulker/shulker_<color.getName()>.png. The 16 color keys above are listed in DyeColor.getId() order (white=0 ... black=15), the order vanilla uses to index SHULKER_TEXTURE_LOCATION. The colored debug path currently tints the shell purple (SHULKER_SHELL) and the head yellow (SHULKER_HEAD); the textured path should drop these tints and sample the per-color texture instead. NOTE on rest pose / animation (not UV-affecting but relevant): vanilla setupAnim sets lid.y = 16 + sin((0.5+peek)*pi)*8 (==24 at peek=0, the bind offset) and head.yRot = (yHeadRot-180-yBodyRot)*pi/180; bbb already mirrors this. The textures are sprites on the shulker_boxes atlas (Sheets.SHULKER_MAPPER, entity/shulker); for the standalone textured render path use the resolved standalone PNG paths above.

specs.vanilla_model_class: net.minecraft.client.model.animal.sniffer.SnifferModel (createBodyLayer; shared by SNIFFER adult and SNIFFER_BABY/snifflet)

cubes.note: texOffs(62,68) addBox(-12.5,-14,-20, 25,29,40, CubeDeformation(0.0)); clean match

INFLATED inner block: vanilla texOffs(62,0) addBox(-12.5,-14,-20, 25,24,40, CubeDeformation(0.5)). The 0.5 inflate makes bbb geometry min -= 0.5 and size += 1 -> bbb [-13,-14.5,-20.5]/[26,25,41]. uv_size stays un-inflated [25,24,40].
texOffs(87,68) addBox(-12.5,12,-20, 25,0,40, CubeDeformation(0.0)); flat belly plane (dy=0)
texOffs(8,15) addBox(-6.5,-7.5,-11.5, 13,18,11, CubeDeformation(0.0)); clean match
texOffs(8,4) addBox(-6.5,7.5,-11.5, 13,0,11, CubeDeformation(0.0)); flat top plane (dy=0)
texOffs(2,0) addBox(0,0,-3, 1,19,7, CubeDeformation(0.0)); clean match
texOffs(48,0) addBox(-1,0,-3, 1,19,7, CubeDeformation(0.0)); clean match (separate texOffs, no mirror() used)
texOffs(10,45) addBox(-6.5,-2,-9, 13,2,9, CubeDeformation(0.0)); SNIFFER_NOSE-colored snout pad; clean match
texOffs(10,57) addBox(-6.5,-7,-8, 13,12,9, CubeDeformation(0.0)); clean match
texOffs(32,87) addBox(-3.5,-1,-4, 7,10,8, CubeDeformation(0.0)); shared SNIFFER_LEG_CUBES box, leg-specific texOffs
texOffs(32,105) addBox(-3.5,-1,-4, 7,10,8, CubeDeformation(0.0)); shared leg box
texOffs(32,123) addBox(-3.5,-1,-4, 7,10,8, CubeDeformation(0.0)); shared leg box
texOffs(0,87) addBox(-3.5,-1,-4, 7,10,8, CubeDeformation(0.0)); shared leg box
texOffs(0,105) addBox(-3.5,-1,-4, 7,10,8, CubeDeformation(0.0)); shared leg box
texOffs(0,123) addBox(-3.5,-1,-4, 7,10,8, CubeDeformation(0.0)); shared leg box

specs.issues: Every bbb cube matches a vanilla addBox cleanly by offset+dims. Part cube counts equal the bbb file exactly: body=3, head=2, left_ear=1, right_ear=1, nose=1, lower_beak=1, and 6 legs x1 = 15 cubes total. NO mirror() is ever called in createBodyLayer, so all mirror=false (left/right ears and the 3 left legs get their own distinct texOffs rather than a mirrored share). DEFORMATION: body cube #2 is the only inflated box -- vanilla addBox(...,25,24,40, CubeDeformation(0.5)); the bbb file pre-baked the 0.5 inflate into geometry (min -= 0.5, size += 1 -> [-13,-14.5,-20.5]/[26,25,41]), but the UV unwrap uses the un-inflated [25,24,40] at texOffs(62,0) -- pass uv_size=[25,24,40], not bbb_size. Two flat planes (body belly dy=0 at texOffs(87,68); head top dy=0 at texOffs(8,4)) -- these are single-quad UVs. BABY: the vanilla SnifferRenderer uses the SAME SnifferModel/createBodyLayer mesh (192x192) for both the adult (ModelLayers.SNIFFER -> sniffer.png) and the baby/snifflet (ModelLayers.SNIFFER_BABY -> snifflet.png); there is no createBabyLayer, the baby is just scaled by AgeableMobRenderer (constructor scale 1.1F passed to super) and re-textured -- so adult and baby share identical UVs/cube layout, only the texture path differs. NOTE: bbb's EntityModelKind::Sniffer is a single unit variant (no {baby} flag, unlike Bee/Dolphin), so bbb currently models adult only; the 'baby' texture variant is included for completeness since the mesh is identical and a future baby split would reuse all the same cubes/UVs.

cubes.note: Vanilla createBodyLayer body: texOffs(0,0).addBox(-1.5,-1.0,0.0, 3.0,2.0,3.0). No CubeDeformation, no mirror. Exact offset+size match.
Vanilla createBodyLayer tail: texOffs(0,0).addBox(0.0,-1.0,0.0, 0.0,2.0,7.0) — a flat 0-thickness fin plane (dx=0). No CubeDeformation, no mirror. Exact offset+size match.

specs.issues: Single texture path only (textures/entity/tadpole/tadpole.png); no variants and no separate baby layer (TadpoleModel defines only createBodyLayer, never a createBabyLayer/createBabyBodyLayer), so baby_atlas_size is reported identical to adult [16,16] and variant_key is "default" — matching bbb selection.rs which returns the single key "tadpole". Both boxes share texOffs(0,0) in vanilla; the two cubes occupy different UV regions because they have different dimensions (3x2x3 body vs 0x2x7 tail fin plane), but the unwrap origin is the same (0,0). The tail cube has dx=0 (a single flat quad plane, double-sided cutout via RenderTypes::entityCutout), which is expected — uv_size x = 0.0. Renderer is a plain MobRenderer with shadowRadius 0.14F and no transform/scale overrides. No CubeDeformation/inflate on either box, so uv_size equals bbb_size for both. Both cubes matched cleanly; part cube counts equal the bbb file (body=1, tail=1).

cubes.note: Vanilla pole.addBox(-0.5,2.0,-0.5, 1,25,1) texOffs(0,6). Exact match. Root part of the mesh; no deformation.
Vanilla base.addBox(-1.5,0.0,-0.5, 3,2,1) texOffs(4,0). Child of pole at ZERO. Exact match.
Vanilla left_spike.addBox(-2.5,-3.0,-0.5, 1,4,1) texOffs(4,3). Child of pole at ZERO. Exact match.
Vanilla middle_spike.addBox(-0.5,-4.0,-0.5, 1,4,1) texOffs(0,0). Child of pole at ZERO. Exact match.

Vanilla right_spike.texOffs(4,3).mirror().addBox(1.5,-3.0,-0.5, 1,4,1). Shares the same texOffs(4,3) as left_spike but with mirror() ACTIVE, so the UV is sampled left-right flipped. Exact geometry match; the bbb file comment already notes the right spike is mirrored on the atlas only.

specs.issues: All 5 bbb cubes match vanilla TridentModel.createLayer() addBox calls exactly by offset and size, in file order (pole / base / left_spike / middle_spike / right_spike). No baby layer (Model<Unit>, projectile thrown trident only), so baby_atlas_size is omitted. Single texture, single variant -> variant_key "default" = textures/entity/trident/trident.png (TridentModel.TEXTURE == ThrownTridentRenderer.TRIDENT_LOCATION). No CubeDeformation/inflate on any box, so uv_size == bbb_size for every cube. Mirror subtlety: left_spike and right_spike both use texOffs(4,3); they share the same 4-wide-by-... atlas region but right_spike has mirror() active, so its UV is horizontally flipped relative to left_spike — the application must pass mirror=true ONLY for right_spike. Overlay note: ThrownTridentRenderer adds a second enchant-foil pass (order 1) when state.isFoil, using getFoilRenderType over the same TRIDENT_LOCATION model — this is an additive glint overlay (no separate texture path) and is deferred per the bbb header comment; it does not affect base UV mapping. Renderer orientation Ry(yRot-90) then Rz(xRot+90) is a model-root transform, not a UV concern. bbb EntityModelKind variant: Self::Trident -> key string \"trident\" in selection.rs (single variant, no baby/color split).
